subject: English Language Arts

secondary_architecture_support.note: Secondary architecture support is present for condensed adaptation, but the multi-day inquiry sequence remains primary.

materials_control_note: Inquiry materials and checkpoint materials remain teacher-controlled until the checkpoint release point.

lesson_overview.overview: Students investigate a local change question across two class days, collect evidence in a task sheet, and convert that evidence into a final written response only after the checkpoint release point.
lesson_overview.essential_question: How can we use gathered evidence to explain a change that matters to our community?

sequence.focus: Launch the inquiry, collect evidence, and complete the checkpoint conversation.
sequence.carryover: Student evidence from the task sheet continues into Day 2.

sequence.focus: Re-open the inquiry, continue evidence gathering, and complete the final response in the declared evidence location.
sequence.carryover: Day 1 evidence must stay available while students draft the final response.

integrity_checks: Teacher-only guidance stays in teacher-facing outputs.
Student task work stays in the task sheet until the final response release point.
Final evidence stays in the final response sheet, not in slide notes or teacher materials.

teacher_guide.big_idea: A multi-day inquiry sequence must preserve where supports live, where checkpoints happen, and where final evidence is recorded.
learning_goals: I can guide students through a two-day inquiry without collapsing teacher and student materials together.
I can use a checkpoint before releasing the final response task.
I can keep final evidence in the declared student-facing artifact.

timing.activity: Launch the inquiry question and clarify the task-sheet purpose.
Students collect and sort evidence in the task sheet.
Teacher runs checkpoint conversations using the checkpoint sheet.
Re-open the inquiry and continue evidence collection.
Students draft the final response in the final response sheet.

teacher_notes: Do not release the final response sheet on Day 1.
Keep embedded supports inside the task sheet rather than moving them to a separate handout.
Use the checkpoint to verify readiness before students begin the final response.

days.carryover_note: Student task work continues into Day 2.

content.subtitle: Launch the question, gather evidence, and get ready for the checkpoint.

content.scenario: A community space is changing. How can we explain what is changing, who is affected, and what evidence matters most?
prompts: Who might be affected by this change?
What evidence would help you explain it clearly?

rows: Record the question and first observations in the task sheet.
Collect at least three pieces of evidence or examples.
Use the checkpoint to confirm you are ready for Day 2 writing.

content.task: Use your task sheet to collect evidence you can carry into Day 2.
prompts: What details show the change clearly?

task_sheet.title: Day 1 Task Sheet
instructions: Write the inquiry question in your own words.
Collect evidence you can use again on Day 2.
Keep your notes clear enough that another reader could follow your thinking.

tasks.label: Part A
tasks.prompt: State the change you are investigating and explain why it matters.

tasks.label: Part B
tasks.prompt: Record three pieces of evidence, details, or examples that help explain the change.

tasks.label: Part C
tasks.prompt: List one question you still need to answer before writing your final response.

embedded_supports: Use point-form notes before writing full sentences.
Underline the strongest piece of evidence in your notes.
Keep all Day 1 evidence on this sheet so it carries forward into Day 2.
success_criteria: I recorded a clear inquiry focus.
I kept my notes in the declared task-sheet location.

checkpoint_sheet.title: Day 1 Checkpoint Sheet
checkpoint_sheet.checkpoint_focus: Teacher checkpoint before Day 2 final response release.
look_fors: Student has a clear inquiry focus.
Student has at least three usable details or examples.
Student can explain which evidence is strongest and why.
conference_prompts: Show me the evidence you plan to use tomorrow.
Which detail is strongest right now?
What still needs clarification before you draft your final response?
checkpoint_sheet.release_rule: Release the Day 2 final response sheet only when the student can point to usable evidence in the task sheet.

days.carryover_note: Continue task-sheet evidence from Day 1 before final response completion.

content.subtitle: Return to your evidence, strengthen your explanation, and complete the final response.

content.scenario: Before you draft, review the notes you carried from Day 1.
prompts: What needs one more detail or explanation?
How will you organize your final response?

rows: Review Day 1 evidence before adding anything new.
Use the task sheet to refine details and wording.
Write the final response only in the final response sheet.

prompts: Which evidence made your explanation stronger?
What changed between Day 1 notes and Day 2 writing?
How did you keep your final evidence in the right place?

task_sheet.title: Day 2 Task Sheet
instructions: Re-read your Day 1 notes before adding anything new.
Clarify wording, add one missing detail, and prepare to draft.
Do not move your final response onto this sheet.

tasks.label: Part A
tasks.prompt: Review your strongest evidence from Day 1 and explain why you are keeping it.

tasks.label: Part B
tasks.prompt: Add one more detail, example, or clarification that strengthens your explanation.

embedded_supports: Box the evidence you will definitely use in your final response.
Cross out weak details instead of deleting the whole record.
Keep this sheet as planning evidence, not the final evidence location.
success_criteria: I improved the evidence I collected earlier.
I know which details belong in my final response.
I kept planning work separate from final evidence.

final_response_sheet.title: Day 2 Final Response Sheet
final_response_sheet.prompt: Use your carried evidence to explain the change, why it matters, and which details best support your explanation.
planning_reminders: Use evidence from the task sheet.
Explain why the evidence matters, not just what it says.
Keep the final written answer on this sheet.
success_criteria: My response answers the inquiry question.
My response uses evidence from the task sheet.
My final evidence is recorded in the declared final response location.
